turnaround_reason: Enphase stock fell from $73.74 to $25.78 during the solar reset (high rates, inventory destocking, European weakness). Now recovering: Q1 2026 beat estimates, Q2 guidance of $280-310M signals revenue stabilization, and a draft FCC proposal to restrict Chinese energy inverters is a direct market-share tailwind for the US microinverter leader. CEO Badri Kothandaraman personally bought 5,000 shares at $67-68, signaling confidence.
bullish_points: FCC draft proposal to restrict Chinese inverters — direct regulatory tailwind displacing competitors from the US market
Q1 2026 beat: EPS $0.47 vs $0.45 estimate (5.5% surprise); Q2 guidance $280-310M signals stabilization after -20.6% revenue decline
CEO bought 5,000 shares at $67-68 in May 2026 — strong insider signal at higher prices than current $49.84
Three consecutive EPS beats (Q3 2025 +37%, Q4 2025 +21%, Q1 2026 +5.5%) — trajectory improving
Insider net buying: 48K shares net over 6 months across 16 purchase transactions
Cash $931M vs debt $612M — net cash position, strong balance sheet
15 option expiries with liquid OI (25K calls, 26K puts) — flexible positioning
Current ratio 3.8, gross margins 27.2% — financially healthy
AI data center power demand emerging as new TAM for GaN microinverters
Recovering screen mode: 26.7% above 200d MA, 111.6% of 100d MA — momentum confirmed
bearish_points: Revenue still declining at -20.6% YoY — stabilization ≠ growth yet
Trailing PE of 49.7x and P/B of 6.0x — rich valuation for a declining-revenue company
Solar sector sensitive to interest rates — any Fed hawkishness pressures the stock
Short interest 22.1% of float — though this can fuel squeeze on positive news
Q2 earnings July 28 is near-term risk — miss could break the recovery pattern
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (July 28) confirming revenue stabilization + FCC Chinese inverter restriction finalized + potential AI data center microinverter application
catalyst_timing: Q2 2026 earnings July 28, 2026; FCC rule finalization timeline TBD (likely H2 2026)
suggested_trade: Spot position ahead of July 28 earnings, or Jan 2027 $45 calls to capture Q2 results and FCC tailwind with leveraged upside
key_risk: Q2 2026 earnings miss on July 28 — if revenue guidance disappoints, the recovery pattern breaks and stock retests $30s
would_skip_if: Q2 revenue comes in below $280M (low end of guidance); FCC proposal is watered down or withdrawn; interest rates spike
